A fantastic sequel and easily one of the most wholesome & uplifting family films I've seen in ages. It's incredibly charming, gentle, kindhearted, and very funny. Paddington is such a sweet optimistic that tries to see the best in people and the world around him. The movie is very emotionally heartfelt and will put you in a good mood. The movie has a great villain played wonderfully by Hugh Grant, who frames Paddington for his burglary. Thankfully, Paddington's naive innocence allows him to change the prison, not the other way around. Especially with his friendship with the prison chef Knuckles, who's hilariously played by Brendon Gleeson. The movie has great direction, cinematography, and artwork. The movie has great pacing and comedic timing. The movie has a very sweet and heartwarming ending, with everyone coming together in gratitude for everything Paddington has done for them with an adorable final shot. It's such a lovable and whimsical movie that will put you in a good mood.

A heartwarming and beautiful movie that is not only entertaining for kids but for adults as well. There are many funny jokes throughout the movie which are really well done. But the best part is easily the emotional connection between paddington and his aunt Lucy. Which drives the whole movie and makes it so enjoyable to watch. And the ending is so beautiful it can lead to tears. 10/10!

Ok, I'm very sorry, but I must be missing something. This was pitched to me as the 'best' kid's movie, possibly the best movie in a long time. But what I saw was, I mean....not bad? Definitely nowhere close to expectations. I mean you expect a lot when people say things like that. It was...cute. Sweet. Occasionally funny or clever. That's about it. Nothing wrong with it. Just slower and less entertaining than I had hoped. About the same as the first Paddington.

In a world where Disney movies are just not doing it anymore, “Paddington 2” not only is one of the greatest family pictures of all time, but it is one of the greatest sequels of all time. Director Paul King masterfully directs the feel good movie of the decade, and there is nothing not to love here. The film promotes terrific family values, is nonstop funny, and is visually appealing for every frame. Believe the hype because “Paddington 2” is as good as everyone says it is.
Easily the best and most entertaining movie of the year yet!

This is one of the best, heartwarming movies I’ve seen in my entire life, all of the emotions a CGI bear makes you feel is crazy, from smiling and laughing to crying your eyes out, this movie is definitely a must watch 😁
I wouldn't say it's a 99% movie. But it's definitely a well done sequel that, in my opinion, flies high above the first one.

Not as good as the original, but watchable. It's got some great comedy and some good acting, but they try to go for a mystery angle which is ruined when they revealed the villain way too early. This completely kills the tension, but overall... its a decent film.

here is nothing better than paddington 2. Literally nothing. If I had to watch this movie on repeat forever, I would rejoice. This moving picture is the absolute pinnacle of human creation, the very fact that mankind was even capable of creating such a film is beyond the comprehension of even the greatest minds in the universe. Thank you for this movie. It is more than a perfect film, as there are no words that can properly describe how incredible this movie really is, it is not perfect, it is Paddington 2.

Director Paul King sets 'em up and knocks 'em down. I know one thing each about the interests of Mr and Mrs Brown from the first third of the movie and they both get paid off in the final act, Chekhov's extendable ladder is used to full effect: this is tight film-making at its best. More importantly this film has just as big a heart as the first and the little bear who just needs a home is faced with the thing he fears the most. I laughed (Hugh Grant and the way Brendon Gleeson says "marmalade"), I cried (Aunt Lucy and memories of Peru) and I enjoyed every minute. I have and will continue to re-watch this film and so should you.
